caption: A young woman with blonde hair, wearing a blue t-shirt and a dark cardigan, is interviewed outdoors in front of a large solar panel array and a small building. In the first three frames, she is speaking, with on-screen text identifying her as "Erica Bjelland" and "Program Development Specialist." Her shirt features the logo "RREAL" and the phrase "The Power to make a difference" above the words "Rural Renewable Energy Alliance." As the sequence progresses, she continues talking while maintaining a pleasant expression. The scene then cuts to an indoor news studio where a man in a grey suit jacket and a patterned tie sits at a curved desk next to a woman in a black blazer. They are positioned in front of a backdrop featuring a "PBS" logo. This shift in setting moves the perspective from the field interview to the studio hosts.
